DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-22-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS ANNA WONG, LARRY WONG, and JACK WONG DEMURRER TO SUPPLEMENTAL COMPLAINT. Appearances: Philip O'Brien, Esq. Attorney at Law, (415) 827-4605 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Paul A. Conroy, of Allman & Nielsen, (415) 461-2700 appearing for Defendants and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer to (unnumbered) "15th" cause of action for conspiracy is sustained with ten days leave to amend. Cause of action uncertain because all causes of action are alleged against all defendants. Defendant Larry Wong's summary adjudication motion was granted as to the 1st, 2nd, 5th, 8th, 9th and 10th causes of action and may not be revived by alleging conspiracy. Plaintiffs must allege facts that show the formation and operation of a conspiracy, wrongful acts of the conspirators thereto, and damage resulting therefrom. Facts must be alleged with enough clarity to put defendants on notice as to what causes of action they allegedly conspired to commit. A party objecting to a complaint may demurrer and answer at the same time. CCP section 430.30(c). (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbbine, CSR #5797

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash And/Or Limit Business Records Subpoena (Ccp 1987.1)
Off Calendar Jul-14-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Philip O'Brien, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: Katiuska Aburto

No other appearance, tentative ruling is adopted. Plaintiff Katiuska Aburto's motion to quash or modify deposition subpoena is granted in part. The subpoena is modified to only require production of payroll records that indicate the dates of employment, the number of hours worked, and compensation received. The custodian of records shall redact the social security number or other personal identifying information from any records.

Counsel for defendant may not disclose or permit to be disclosed the records produced pursuant to the subpoenas unless specifically permitted to do so by the court after a hearing and a showing of good cause.

Notwithstanding the above, counsel for defendant may disclose or permit to be disclosed the records produced pursuant to the subpoenas to persons employed by counsel to assist in the preparation of the case, if that disclosure is required for that preparation. Persons provided this information by counsel shall be informed that further dissemination of the information constitutes a violation of a court order. Order signed in open court. Commissioner: Bruce Chan. Not reported.
